intent_description: List posts from an Instagram profile, hashtag, or location URL. Returns one row per post with caption, engagement counts, timestamp, media URLs, hashtags, and mentions.
input_notes: username is an array of handles (or profile URLs) even for a single account — extract the handle from the URL path before passing. resultsLimit caps post count (default 50, hard ceiling around 1000 per Apify docs).

intent_description: Get profile-level info for one or more Instagram accounts: bio, follower count, post count, profile picture, verification status. Returns one row per profile. Recent posts come nested inside latestPosts and should not be flattened by default.
input_notes: Takes usernames (plain strings), not URLs. Extract the username from the URL path before passing. usernames is an array even for a single account.

intent_description: List job postings from a LinkedIn Jobs search or collection URL. Returns one row per job with title, company, location, posting date, employment type, and a link to the listing.
input_notes: urls is a plain string array (schema editor=stringList), even for a single URL. Pass a LinkedIn jobs search/collection URL, not an individual profile. The actor's item-count field is 'count' (integer), NOT maxItems. Columns are taken from a real jobs.csv export; the dotted columns (companyAddress.*, salaryInsights.*) are CSV flattenings of nested objects and will not populate via the top-level shaper, so default_columns are all functional top-level fields.

intent_description: Scrape Amazon product listings from a search, category, or product URL. Returns one row per product with title, brand, price, rating, review count, and a link to the product page.
input_notes: Required field is categoryOrProductUrls (schema editor=requestListSources): an array of {"url": ...} objects, NOT plain strings and NOT startUrls. The per-start-URL cap is maxItemsPerStartUrl (integer). Columns are the real top-level keys from a captured sample row; nested fields are kept top-level rather than flattened with dotted paths because the shaper projects by top-level key only (starsBreakdown/visitStoreLink/aiReviewsSummary are dicts; attributes/productOverview/productPageReviews are lists of objects). Note price/listPrice/seller can be null for some products.
